description: Create a two-column content slide on small and mid-market attractiveness with a left-side list of five advantages and a right-side vertical bar chart showing top quartile IRR by fund size.

header.headline: The small & mid-market exhibits attractive characteristics that can lead to potential outperformance

items.sub: 10x greater opportunity set than the larger segments of the market
Talent, Technology and M&A can lead to significant growth and value creation
20% less leverage than industry average large-cap deals
10% lower than industry median large-cap deals
Limited reliance on IPO Market with dynamic ability to sell to strategics and larger private equity firms

sidebar.title: Potential Outperformance
sidebar.body: Top quartile small & mid-market returns exceed larger funds by about 5%

footer.source: 1 Source: FactSet as of 2/6/2025. 2 Source: Cambridge Associates LLC Private Investments Database (as reported by investment managers), FactSet Research Systems, and Frank Russell Company. Reflecting averages as of 12/31/2020, and outliers were identified and excluded from calculations. Includes unrealized and realized US-based buyout and growth equity companies acquired between 2000 and 2020. USPE counts vary by metric and size ranging from 677 to 4,938 companies. Company size based on enterprise value at acquisition. Small cap = enterprise values of less than $250 million, mid cap = enterprise values from $250 million to $1 billion, and large cap = enterprise values greater than $1 billion. 3 Source: Pitchbook. Median Transaction Value / EBITDA Multiples of US buyout deals occurring between 2010 and 6/30/2024. 4 Source: MSCI Private I. Top Quartile IRR of U.S. and European buyout funds with vintages between 2011 and 2021 as of 12/31/2025.
